steps.title: Turn the wording into two points
steps.content: The problem gives the initial value $(0,70)$. A $50\%$ increase means multiply by $1.50$, so when $x=1$, $$y=70(1.50)=105.$$

steps.title: Find the exponential model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y=a(b)^x$. The starting value is $a=70$, and the growth multiplier is $b=1.50$, so $$y=70(1.50)^x.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A is the only option with initial value $70$ and multiplier $1.50$. Choice B is decay, and choices C and D start at $50$ instead of $70$, so choice A is correct.
